zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Beatrix Alice Stewart Of Crawford於1268年1月出生在蘇格蘭艾斯爾登東納爾德時，她的父親Alexander Stewart 4th High Steward Of Scotland54歲，母親Jean MacRory Countess Bute Marchioness59歲。她於1289年與Alexander Lindsay Lord of Barnweill在蘇格蘭藍納可爾克勞福德結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她於1324年住在蘇格蘭雷福福爾厄斯金。她去世於1337年在蘇格蘭藍納可爾克勞福德享年69歲，埋葬在蘇格蘭艾斯爾。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lady Beatrix Alice Stewart Of Crawford于1268年1月出生在苏格兰艾斯爾登東納爾德时，她的父亲Alexander Stewart 4th High Steward Of Scotland54岁，母亲Jean MacRory Countess Bute Marchioness59岁。她于1289年与Alexander Lindsay Lord of Barnweill在苏格兰藍納可爾克勞福德结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她于1324年住在苏格兰雷福福爾厄斯金。她去世于1337年在苏格兰藍納可爾克勞福德享年69岁，埋葬在苏格兰艾斯爾。

nameMeanings.name: Stewart
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish (Lanarkshire) and English: originally an occupational name for an administrative official of an estate, from Middle English stiward, Old English stigweard, stīweard, a compound of stig ‘house(hold)’ + weard ‘guardian’. In the Anglo-Saxon period this title was used of an officer controlling the domestic affairs of a household, especially of the royal household; after the Norman Conquest it was also used more widely as the native equivalent of Seneschal, for the steward of a manor or manager of an estate. In Scotland the term was also used of a magistrate originally appointed by the king to administer crown lands, forming a stewartry.

History: Stuart or Stewart is the surname of one of the great families of Scotland, the royal family of Scotland from the 14th century, and of England from 1603, when James VI of Scotland acceded to the English throne as James I. There were many minor branches of the family left in Britain after the flight of James II in 1688, but not every bearer of the surname can claim relationship with the royal house, even in Scotland. Every great house in medieval England and Scotland had its steward, and in many cases the office gave rise to a hereditary surname. The fall of the house of Stuart in Britain, conversely, led to the establishment of several highly placed branches bearing this surname in continental Europe, which are in most cases related to the old Scottish royal family.

variants.name: Stuart
meanings: Scottish (Aberdeenshire and Banffshire): variant of   Stewart  . This French form of the name, there being no W in the French language, was adopted by Mary, Queen of Scots, on her return to Scotland in 1561 and has since come to be more popular among royalists than the earlier spellings.
History: Stuart or Stewart is the surname of one of the great families of Scotland (see note at   Stewart  ).

variants.name: Stewardson
meanings: English (Lancashire and Westmorland): patronymic from Middle English stiward ‘steward’ (see Stewart ) + -son ‘son of the steward’.

variants.name: Steward
meanings: English (London and East Anglia): variant of   Stewart  .

variants.name: Steuart
meanings: Scottish: variant of   Stewart  .

nameMeanings.name: Of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Cheng, Jing, Kam Fung, Wai Shing, Wing, Yali, Yuk.
Chinese:
variant Romanization of the surnames 歐 and 區, see   Ou   1 and 2.

variants.name: Ou
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ying, Ching, Heng, Hong, Jia, Kuang Yu, Li-Ching, Cho, Chong, Chung, Sa.  Southeast Asian Lichun, Ming, Quan, Roeun, Sokhom, Chau, Hai, Leang, Ly, Nhanh, Phan, Pho, Tha.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 歐, meaning ‘to sing someone's praise’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the placename Dong Ou (東甌), a fief (in present-day Wenzhou in Zhejiang province) which was granted to Yao in 192  BC . It is said that Yao was a descendant of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from the placename Ou Yu (歐餘), the name of a fief (located in present-day Zhejiang province) that was granted to Ti, the second son of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 區, originally denoting an ancient vessel for measurement in ancient Chinese: from the personal name of Ou Zhizi (歐治子), an expert sword-maker who lived during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ). Some of his descendants inherited Ou (歐) as their surname, simplifying the Chinese character 歐 to 區.

nameMeanings.name: Crawford
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish and English: habitational name from any of various places called Crawford, primarily the one in Lanarkshire (Scotland), and possibly also from the one in Lancashire. Both are named in Old English with crāwe ‘crow’ + ford ‘ford’.

variants.name: Crafford
meanings: English (Cumberland) and Scottish: variant of   Crawford  .

variants.name: Crofoot
meanings: Scottish: variant of   Crawford  .

English: variant ofCrowfoot, a nickname for someone with splayed feet or someother deformity of the foot, from Old English crāwe ‘crow’ +fōt ‘foot’. In Middle English crou-fot also denotedthe buttercup, and it may be from this sense that the name arose,although the reason for its adoption is unclear.

variants.name: Crofut
meanings: Scottish and English: variant of Crawford or Crowfoot (see Crofoot ).

nameMeanings.name: Beatrix
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From a Late Latin personal name, which was borne by a saint martyred in Rome, together with Saints Faustinus and Simplicius, in the early 4th century. The original form of the name was probably Viatrix, a feminine version of Viator ‘voyager (through life)’, which was a favourite name among early Christians. This was then altered by association with Latin Beatus ‘blessed’ (Via- and Bea- sometimes being pronounced the same in Late Latin). See also Beatrice .

nameMeanings.name: Alice
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a variant of Adelaide , representing an Old French spelling of a reduced form of Germanic Adalheidis. Alice and Adelaide were already regarded as distinct names in English during the medieval period. Alice enjoyed a surge of popularity in the 19th century and periods of favour ever since. It was the name of the central character of Lewis Carroll's Alice's Adventures in Wonderland ( 1865 ) and Through the Looking Glass ( 1872 ), who was based on his child friend Alice Liddell , daughter of the Dean of Christ Church, Oxford.

variants.name: Alys
meanings: Variant spelling of   Alice .

variants.name: Alis
meanings: equivalent of Alice .

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Beatrix Alice Stewart Of Crawford was born in January 1268, in Dundonald, Ayrshire, Scotland, her father, Alexander Stewart 4th High Steward Of Scotland, was 54 and her mother, Jean MacRory Countess Bute Marchioness, was 59. She married Alexander Lindsay Lord of Barnweill in 1289, in Crawford, Lanarkshire, Scotland. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 2 daughters. She lived in Erskine, Renfrewshire, Scotland in 1324. She died in 1337, in Crawford, Lanarkshire, Scotland, at the age of 69, and was buried in Ayrshire, Scotland.

Elle a épousé Alexander Lindsay Lord of Barnweill en 1289, à Crawford, Lanarkshire, Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 2 filles. Elle a vécu à Erskine, Renfrewshire, Écosse en 1324. Elle est décédée en 1337, à Crawford, Lanarkshire, Écosse, à l’âge de 69 ans, et a été enterrée à Ayrshire, Écosse.

ja.lifeSummary: Lady Beatrix Alice Stewart Of Crawfordが1268年1月にスコットランドエイルシュアイアダンドナルドで生まれた時，父Alexander Stewart 4th High Steward Of Scotlandは54歳，母Jean MacRory Countess Bute Marchionessは59歳であった。1289年にスコットランドラナルクシュアイアクラウフォードでAlexander Lindsay Lord of Barnweillと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1324年にはスコットランドレンフリューシュアイアエルスキンに住んでいた。彼女は1337年にスコットランドラナルクシュアイアクラウフォードで69歳で死去し，スコットランドエイルシュアイアにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Beatrix Alice Stewart Of Crawford(이)가 1268년 1월 스컷랜드 에이르실르 던도널드에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Alexander Stewart 4th High Steward Of Scotland(은)는 54세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Jean MacRory Countess Bute Marchioness(은)는 59세였다. 그녀는 1289년 스컷랜드 랜아르크실르 크롤퍼드에서 Alexander Lindsay Lord of Barnweill(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 1324년도에 스컷랜드 렌프레w실르 어스킨에서 살았다. 그녀는 1337년 스컷랜드 랜아르크실르 크롤퍼드에서 69세의 나이로 사망했으며 스컷랜드 에이르실르에 묻혔다.

Ela casou-se com Alexander Lindsay Lord of Barnweill em 1289, em Crawford, Lanarkshire, Scotland. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela viveu em Erskine, Renfrewshire, Scotland em 1324. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lady Beatrix Alice Stewart Of Crawford родилась январь 1268 г. в местности дундональд, аиршир, Шотландия, её отцу по имени Alexander Stewart 4th High Steward Of Scotland было 54 года, а матери, которую звали Jean MacRory Countess Bute Marchioness, 59. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Alexander Lindsay Lord of Barnweill в 1289 г. в местности кравфорд, ланаркшир, Шотландия. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 2 дочери. Она жила в местности эрскаин, ренфрюшир, Шотландия в 1324 году. Она умерла в 1337 г. в местности кравфорд, ланаркшир, Шотландия в возрасте 69 лет и была похоронена в местности аиршир, Шотландия.

Murió en 1337, en Crawford, Lanarkshire, Scotland, a la edad de 69 años, y fue sepultada en Ayrshire, Scotland.
